problem.title: Minimum Window Substring
problem.description: Given two strings s and t of lengths m and n respectively, return the minimum window substring of s such that every character in t (including duplicates) is included in the window.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Sliding Window
solutions.description: Use two pointers to maintain a window over s and a counter of how many distinct characters in t are fully satisfied. Expand the right pointer to include new characters; whenever all characters of t are covered, shrink from the left to minimize the window, recording the minimum. The key insight is tracking 'formed' — the count of characters that have reached their required frequency — so we know in O(1) when the window is valid.
